title: Tsunami risks in the Mediterranean: why Nice should prepare an evacuation plan
content: The Mediterranean sea is widely perceived as having a low tsunami risk. History and recent modelling technology have demonstrated that destructive waves have already hit the French coast and could do so again.

title: F Words for warning reactions
content: Many other F words are witnessed among people experiencing difficulties, challenges, threats, perils, and dangers. Here, we compile and summarise them for reacting to warnings.

title: Adapting India’s social protection architecture for climate resilience
content: Without climate-responsive triggers in its welfare programmes, India risks allowing extreme weather to repeatedly erase the gains of poverty reduction

title: A warmer climate means bigger hail
content: New attribution research shows how extra heat in the atmosphere can turn thunderstorms into factories for dangerous, softball-size hail.

title: Beyond counting roofs: Why disaster recovery metrics shape the wrong outcomes
content: If we make destroyed homes the global measure of disaster impact, we risk channelling resources toward the most visible part of recovery while neglecting the systems that make four walls and a roof actually livable.

title: Lessons from the Great East Japan Earthquake
content: Fifteen years after 3/11, the anniversary offers an opportunity to reassess the importance of sustained engagement in disaster recovery and what can still be learned from it.

title: 6 Global environments of ageing: towards co-designing climate resilient environments and communities for ageing well
content: This chapter examines how climate change affects older populations and explores ways to design climate-resilient environments and communities that support healthy ageing.

title: Selective forest thinning in the eastern Cascades supports both snowpack and wildfire resilience
content: A team of researchers at the University of Washington and The Nature Conservancy (TNC) embarked on an ambitious, multiyear study of snowpack along Cle Elum Ridge, an area of the eastern Cascades in the headwaters of the Yakima River Basin.

title: Substantial increases in the likelihood of extreme fire weather events for fire-prone ecosystems in Australia
content: Climate change is projected to significantly increase the frequency and intensity of extreme fire weather events across Australia, particularly in the fire-prone eucalyptus forests of southeast Australia.

title: Population exposure predicts flood losses in Sweden
content: This study provides a national-scale assessment of flood exposure in Sweden by integrating high resolution population and building data with inundation extents for the 100-year and highest possible flood scenarios.

title: Wind impact on sea level variability along the US Atlantic and Gulf coasts: implications to coastal flooding hazard
content: The study shows that offshore wind patterns significantly influence sea level variability along the U.S. Atlantic and Gulf coasts and are key drivers of coastal flooding risk.

title: When the storm hits, cash is not a constraint: Jamaica’s approach to disaster financing
content: Recognizing its high exposure to climate and natural hazards, the country developed a multilayered disaster risk financing strategy designed to cushion the fiscal impact of major events like Hurricane Melissa.

title: Four ways to tackle health and climate together – and lift millions of people out of poverty
content: The article outlines four win‑win strategies that improve health and address climate change: phasing out fossil fuels, protecting nature, transforming food systems, and building resilient infrastructure to reduce poverty and boost wellbeing.

title: Webinar: Measuring the results of disaster risk management interventions
content: This webinar will present new IndiKit guidance on key disaster risk management (DRM) indicators.

title: Heavy rains threaten homeless people living in Bay Area stream corridors
content: Heavy, increasingly frequent Bay Area rainstorms are raising flood risks for homeless people living near streams. Researchers mapped socially vulnerable areas to guide warnings, resources, and planning as climate change intensifies extreme weather.

title: Why the planet doesn’t dry out together: Scientists solve a global climate puzzle
content: Warming intensifies droughts – but the planet has a hidden stabiliser. Natural ocean and rainfall cycles prevent severe droughts from synchronising globally, affecting only 1.8–6.5% of land at any time.

title: Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar (IITGN)
content: IIT Gandhinagar is an institution for higher learning in science, technology and related fields, aspiring to develop high quality scientists, engineers, leaders and entrepreneurs to meet the needs of the society - now and in the future.

title: Alaska’s glacial lakes are expanding, increasing the risk of destructive outburst floods
content: As Alaska’s glaciers melt with rising temperatures, expanding glacial lakes are increasing the risk of sudden glacial lake outburst floods, which can rapidly release large volumes of water and threaten downstream communities and ecosystems.

title: U.S. billion-dollar weather and climate disasters in 2025
content: Research by Climate Central shows that during 2025, there were 23 individual weather and climate disasters with at least $1 billion in damages.

title: Spatial heterogeneity in indirect flooding-mitigation benefits of the Three Gorges Project across China
content: This study develops a Spatial Footprint Impact Assessment framework (SFIA), which is applied to the Three Gorges Project (TGP), evaluating the spatial heterogeneity of indirect flood-retention benefits induced by the TGP across 31 provinces in China.

title: From pilot to policy: How governments are leading anticipatory action
content: This article highlights the growing shift toward anticipatory action in humanitarian response, where governments and partners act on forecasts before climate disasters strike rather than responding afterward.

title: Climate-driven changes in wildfire seasonality across North America
content: The study finds that boreal and taiga forests have experienced a clear advance in seasonal wildfire activity, whereas Mediterranean and desert regions show delayed and extended late-season burning.

title: Strengthening inclusive climate and disaster risk financing in the Philippines: A landscape overview of systems, instruments, and entry points
content: This publication outlines the evolving landscape of climate and disaster risk financing and insurance in the Philippines, emphasising its central role in strengthening DRR, food system resilience, and support for vulnerable populations.

title: Heatwaves driving recent ‘surge’ in compound drought and heat extremes
content: Heatwaves are increasingly triggering compound drought‑heat events worldwide. This study shows these “heatwave‑led” extremes have doubled since 2000 due to stronger land‑atmosphere feedbacks, raising risks for crops, ecosystems and public health.

title: Hearing the call: How Timor-Leste is reaching the "last mile" with Early Warnings
content: An important study set out to understand the real-world reach of Timor-Leste's early warning systems by surveying 909 households and conducting 27 focus group discussions across eight municipalities.

title: Latest extreme heat additions in the Knowledge Base
content:
Latest extreme heat additions in the Knowledge Base



A heatwave is a marked warming of the air, or the invasion of very warm air, over a large area; it usually lasts from a few days to a few weeks (

title: Building disaster resilient communities through women’s leadership
content: This research identifies the barriers preventing women’s participation and leadership in DPR and assesses how women’s participation and leadership strengthen overall community resilience to disasters caused by natural hazards.

title: In conversation with Dr. Katie Kuschminder about women, climate and human mobility
content: We had a chat with Dr. Katie Kuschminder and talked about how climate risks influence the way people move and why women’s experiences within those movements matter and should be heard.

title: Seminar: Long term disaster impacts on education outcomes
content: This webinar explores how disasters and climate extremes affect education outcomes, and why this is an important but often overlooked consideration in disaster risk reduction, resilience and recovery.

title: Working with indigenous knowledge holders to advance equitable and inclusive nature and climate initiatives
content: The article provides guidance on how to engage ethically and collaboratively with Indigenous Knowledge holders to support inclusive nature-based climate adaptation and biodiversity initiatives.

title: Our partners
content:
Our Partners 



A growing number of organizations are advancing research, policy, and practice to address the rising risks of extreme heat. The partners highlighted below contribute knowledge, tools, and initiatives that help strengthen heat risk governance and support more effective preparedness. Their work complements the resources featured on the Exteme Heat Portal and helps accelerate collective action on heat resilience.

title: Extreme Heat Working Group Session at HNPW 26
content: The 2026 HNPW Extreme Heat session builds on similar sessions in past HNPW meetings to share knowledge on shelter-based options to address extreme heat and identify key action points for the Working Group through the next 12 months.

title: 7th World Landslide Forum India 2026
content: The 7th World Landslide Forum (WLF7) will be held from 23–27 November 2026 at Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Faridabad (Delhi), India.

title: Guidelines for simulation exercises on early warning systems for weather induced risks
content: Guidelines offering a practical, adaptable framework to design and run simulation exercises that test and strengthen Early Warning Systems, improving decision‑making, coordination, and timely early action.

title: GEOGLAM crop monitor for early warning report: March 2026
content: The March 2026 GEOGLAM Crop Monitor for Early Warning report provides an overview of global crop conditions with a focus on regions vulnerable to food insecurity.

title: Two Internships in Disaster Risk Reduction and Climate Change Adaptation
content: We seek two interns to join our team remotely to support our efforts in addressing global DRR and policy-related challenges.

title: Leading the change: Women shaping a sustainable future
content: Women are not merely beneficiaries of sustainable development; they are designing its blueprints.

title: Global stockpile for health emergency response: A distributed strategy for pandemic preparedness in a post–COVID 19, fragmenting world
content: The paper argues that COVID‑19 exposed major weaknesses in global health preparedness and proposes a distributed, multi‑actor global stockpile system to ensure faster, fairer access to essential medical supplies during future pandemics.

title: Investing in inclusion: Why we need gender-responsive climate finance
content: Across Asia and the Pacific, change acts as a "threat multiplier," deepening pre-existing inequalities and disproportionately impacting women during disasters.

title: Transforming community disaster preparedness: PHEDM model evaluation in Uttarakhand, India
content: The study evaluates the PHEDM model in two Uttarakhand villages, showing that community‑level training and structured risk assessment significantly improved disaster preparedness, adaptive capacity and emergency response compared with the control village.
